req.title: Announcement rollout
req.statement: A system-admin shall publish an org-wide announcement to employees in chosen departments; publishing without any department selection shall be rejected.

doc.reasoning: The requirement has three distinct test-worthy behaviors: (1) happy-path publication with at least one department selected (EP – valid partition), (2) rejection when zero departments are selected (NEG / DT – error pathway yields 422), and (3) the boundary condition of selecting exactly one department – the minimum valid cardinality (BVA – lower boundary of the 'departments' set). A fourth test covers the state-transition from draft to published to confirm the announcement becomes visible to target employees after submission (STC). Each test is self-contained via fixtures that create and clean up their own data.

tests.name: test_publish_announcement_multiple_departments
preconditions: At least two departments exist in the organisation
actions: Fill announcement title with a unique string
Select two or more departments from the department picker
expected: Success confirmation message is displayed on the UI
Announcement appears in the org-announcements list
trace: R-WEB-087: positive outcome – announcement created for chosen departments
tests.executable:
import pytest
from playwright.sync_api import expect

def test_publish_announcement_multiple_departments(admin_page, unique_title, dept_ids):
    page = admin_page
    page.goto("/org-announcements/new")

    page.fill("[data-testid='announcement-title']", unique_title)
    page.fill("[data-testid='announcement-body']", "Body text for EP test")

    for dept_id in dept_ids[:2]:
        page.check(f"[data-testid='dept-checkbox-{dept_id}']")

    with page.expect_response(
        lambda r: "/org-announcements" in r.url and r.request.method == "POST"
    ) as resp_info:
        page.click("[data-testid='publish-btn']")

    resp = resp_info.value
    assert resp.status in (200, 201), f"Expected 200/201, got {resp.status}"

    expect(page.locator("[data-testid='success-banner']")).to_be_visible()

    page.goto("/org-announcements")
    expect(page.locator(f"text={unique_title}")).to_be_visible()

tests.name: test_publish_announcement_no_departments_rejected
actions: Fill announcement title with a unique string
expected: Validation error message referencing department selection is displayed
trace: R-WEB-087: error pathway – 422 when no departments selected
tests.executable:
import pytest
from playwright.sync_api import expect

def test_publish_announcement_no_departments_rejected(admin_page, unique_title):
    page = admin_page
    page.goto("/org-announcements/new")

    page.fill("[data-testid='announcement-title']", unique_title)
    page.fill("[data-testid='announcement-body']", "Body text for NEG test")

    # Ensure no departments are checked
    for cb in page.locator("[data-testid^='dept-checkbox-']").all():
        if cb.is_checked():
            cb.uncheck()

    with page.expect_response(
        lambda r: "/org-announcements" in r.url and r.request.method == "POST"
    ) as resp_info:
        page.click("[data-testid='publish-btn']")

    resp = resp_info.value
    assert resp.status == 422, f"Expected 422, got {resp.status}"

    expect(page.locator("[data-testid='dept-error-msg']")).to_be_visible()
    assert "/org-announcements/new" in page.url

tests.name: test_publish_announcement_exactly_one_department
preconditions: At least one department exists in the organisation
actions: Fill announcement title with a unique string
Select exactly one department from the department picker
expected: Success confirmation message is displayed on the UI
Announcement is associated with exactly one department in the response payload
trace: R-WEB-087: boundary – minimum valid department count is 1
tests.executable:
import pytest
from playwright.sync_api import expect
import json

def test_publish_announcement_exactly_one_department(admin_page, unique_title, dept_ids):
    page = admin_page
    page.goto("/org-announcements/new")

    page.fill("[data-testid='announcement-title']", unique_title)
    page.fill("[data-testid='announcement-body']", "Body text for BVA test")

    # Select only the first department (lower boundary = 1)
    page.check(f"[data-testid='dept-checkbox-{dept_ids[0]}']")

    with page.expect_response(
        lambda r: "/org-announcements" in r.url and r.request.method == "POST"
    ) as resp_info:
        page.click("[data-testid='publish-btn']")

    resp = resp_info.value
    assert resp.status in (200, 201), f"Expected 200/201, got {resp.status}"

    body = resp.json()
    departments = body.get("departments", [])
    assert len(departments) == 1, f"Expected 1 department, got {len(departments)}"

    expect(page.locator("[data-testid='success-banner']")).to_be_visible()

tests.name: test_announcement_state_transitions_draft_to_published
preconditions: At least one department exists in the organisation
A draft announcement exists (state=DRAFT) for the admin's organisation
actions: Navigate to the existing draft announcement edit page
Click Publish to transition state from DRAFT to PUBLISHED
Verify announcement list reflects PUBLISHED state
Verify target employees see the notification
expected: Announcement state changes from DRAFT to PUBLISHED
Status badge on list page shows PUBLISHED
trace: R-WEB-087: state transition – draft → published upon valid publish action
R-WEB-087: expected outcome – target employees notified
tests.executable:
import pytest
from playwright.sync_api import expect

def test_announcement_state_transitions_draft_to_published(
    admin_page, draft_announcement, dept_ids, employee_api_client
):
    page = admin_page
    ann_id = draft_announcement["id"]
    page.goto(f"/org-announcements/{ann_id}/edit")

    page.check(f"[data-testid='dept-checkbox-{dept_ids[0]}']")

    with page.expect_response(
        lambda r: f"/org-announcements/{ann_id}" in r.url
        and r.request.method in ("PUT", "PATCH", "POST")
    ) as resp_info:
        page.click("[data-testid='publish-btn']")

    resp = resp_info.value
    assert resp.status in (200, 201)
    body = resp.json()
    assert body.get("state") == "PUBLISHED", f"State mismatch: {body.get('state')}"

    page.goto("/org-announcements")
    row = page.locator(f"[data-testid='announcement-row-{ann_id}']")
    expect(row.locator("[data-testid='status-badge']")).to_have_text("PUBLISHED")

    notif_resp = employee_api_client.get(f"/notifications?announcement_id={ann_id}")
    assert notif_resp.status_code == 200
    assert len(notif_resp.json().get("notifications", [])) > 0
